truthful bid maximizes utility in this fixed-bid scan
truthful bid is not utility-maximizing in this fixed-bid scan

notes: DIM and PRM scans reuse the realized bid book and recompute winners/payments with all non-target bids fixed.
PRM dynamic preference updates are not re-simulated for each report; this is an ex-post implementation audit.
TRAIM scans multiply one base station's reported cost for allocation/payment while holding physical coverage and true costs fixed.

audit_notes.note: Simulation outputs are aggregated directly; calibration and forced ordering post-processing have been removed.
Utility plot fields use display_value = raw_value / 100; raw utility fields are preserved as *_raw.
Primary DIM runs use strategy=F; Figure 3-8 still reports F/R/RF strategy comparisons.
DIM-derived rounds may allow secondary positive real-task bids when dim_allow_secondary_positive_bids=true; these bids are generated before allocation and are part of the raw bid book.
Figure 3-6 plot fields use raw bid intensity for A/B/goal/compete and paired decoy-influenced node counts for tau_decoy; raw selection counts are preserved as tau_*_raw.
Figure 4-2 uses raw participation intensity; unique participant counts are preserved as *_unique_participants_raw.
Figure 4-5 uses common-random-number task prefixes and reports standard errors; no monotonic smoothing is applied.
Task-level bid/price heatmaps use the first repeat at comparison_node_count, not a ranking-optimized representative sample.
